title: Everything to know about Lake Merced, a beloved SF spot
content: # Everything to know about San Francisco's Lake Merced. While the park's large lake doesn't allow swimming—you can boat and fish though—it does offer one of the best running trails in San Francisco, as well as golf courses and birdwatching. If you haven't visited Lake Merced before or it's been a minute, read on for the best things to see and do at this beloved spot. The park is run by San Francisco Recreation and Parks, with a section devoted to the restoration and protection of habitat for birds. The lake itself is actually made up of four sections: North Lake and East Lake (even though these two are connected by a small inlet and a bridge), the large main South Lake and the smaller Impound Lake at the south end, which is a popular spot to look for birds.

title: Lake Merced
content: # Lake Merced. Lake Merced, located on the outskirts of San Francisco near Ocean Beach and Daly City, is a major fresh water, recreational, and natural resource for the City and County of San Francisco and the surrounding area. More than half of the park is managed by the Recreation and Parks Department’s Natural Areas Program, which works to restore and protect habitat for birds and other wildlife, as well as to maintain and improve trails. The San Francisco Public Utilities Commission (SFPUC) manages Lake Merced, a beautiful, coastal freshwater lake in the Southwest corner of San Francisco that is an important recreational and environmental asset and resource for the City and the entire Bay Area. The SFPUC is coordinating with San Francisco Recreation and Park Department as well as the local Regional Water Quality Control Board (RWQCB), to keep the public notified of conditions in Lake Merced.
